title_en: A mystery street artist is taking over cities
title_zh: 一位神秘的街头艺术家正在占领各大城市
rubric_en: You can hunt for his mosaics in Paris and beyond
rubric_zh: 你可以在巴黎等地追寻他的马赛克作品

paragraphs.en: STANDING AT THE top of the Eiffel Tower, almost everyone looks down. Tourists jostle to peer at Paris’s famous landmarks and the snaking Seine. Yet those in the know gaze upwards: they climb to the highest public platform to view not the metropolis, but a mosaic. Up in one nook is a small pixelated artwork made from ceramic tiles, which depicts a blue creature with a cloud.
paragraphs.zh: 站在埃菲尔铁塔顶端时，几乎每个人都会向下望去。游客们挤在一起，想要一睹巴黎著名地标和蜿蜒流淌的塞纳河。然而，真正懂行的人会抬头看去：他们爬上最高的公众观景平台，不是去看这座大都市，而是去看一幅马赛克。高处的一个角落里有一件由陶瓷瓷砖拼成的小型像素画，描绘的是一只头顶乌云的蓝色生物。

paragraphs.en: Invader, the Frenchman behind the mosaic, is one of the most prolific street artists in the world. (Yes, he is even busier than Banksy.) He has planted more than 1,500 works across Paris alone. There are thousands more such “invasions” in over 80 cities around the world, from New York to Tokyo. His pixelated pictures have been affixed to the Hollywood sign and a ski lift in the Swiss Alps; they lie at the bottom of the ocean near Cancún and orbit Earth in the International Space Station (pictured above).
paragraphs.zh: 这位马赛克背后的人，法国艺术家“Invader”，是世界上最多产的街头艺术家之一。（是的，他甚至比Banksy还忙。）仅在巴黎，他就已经“入侵”了1500多件作品。世界各地还有更多“入侵”作品，遍及80多个城市，从纽约到东京。

paragraphs.en: Like any successful intruder, he often works under cover of darkness. (Invader has installed almost every piece himself, from Miami to Marrakech.) And, like any serious street artist, his identity is a secret. That hasn’t stopped him from gaining fans. If you know a child in a city that has been invaded, chances are you’ve heard of FlashInvaders, an app on which you score points for finding (or “flashing”) the mosaics. The game has more than 550,000 players, up from about 360,000 in 2024. Players have logged some 47m flashes to date.
paragraphs.zh: 和任何成功的闯入者一样，他常常在夜色掩护下行动。（Invader几乎每一件作品都亲自安装，从迈阿密到马拉喀什。）和任何严肃的街头艺术家一样，他的身份一直是秘密。但这并没有阻止他赢得粉丝。若你认识某个曾经被“入侵”的城市里的孩子，你很可能听说过FlashInvaders这个应用，玩家可以在其中通过找到（或“闪现”）这些马赛克来得分。该游戏目前已有超过55万名玩家，高于2024年的约36万。玩家累计记录了约4700万次“闪现”。

paragraphs.en: Invader uses the ancient practice of mosaic to create figures inspired by retro video games. Each tile acts as a “perfect pixel”, as Rafael Schacter, a street-art expert at University College London puts it, which makes the mosaics look like digital images. Many are variations of the crabs and UFOs found in “Space Invaders” (1978). Others reflect their surroundings, as with a fox drinking a cup of tea in London.
paragraphs.zh: Invader运用古老的马赛克技艺来创作受复古电子游戏启发的人物形象。正如伦敦大学学院的街头艺术专家拉斐尔·沙克特（Rafael Schacter）所说，每一块瓷砖都像是一个“完美像素”，因此这些马赛克看起来就像数字图像。许多作品是《太空侵略者》（1978）里螃蟹和UFO的变体。也有一些作品会呼应当地环境，比如在伦敦就有一只喝茶的狐狸。

paragraphs.en: The pixels also mean the images can be scanned on a phone, a bit like a QR code, to play FlashInvaders. The aim of the game is not only to interact with fans, says Invader—who spoke to The Economist via email to stay anonymous—but also to spark curiosity and urban dérive (exploration). Searching for the mosaics takes you to parts of cities you wouldn’t normally see. The game can require detective work, as players must avoid being duped by impostors who try to mimic Invader’s style.
paragraphs.zh: 这些像素也意味着这些图像可以像二维码一样用手机扫描，从而玩“FlashInvaders”。这款游戏的目的不仅是与粉丝互动，Invader——他通过电子邮件接受《经济学人》采访以保护身份——还想激发好奇心和“城市漫游式”探索。寻找马赛克会带你去到那些你平时不会去的城市角落。游戏有时甚至需要侦探式推理，因为玩家必须避免被那些试图模仿 Invader 风格的冒牌者误导。

paragraphs.en: FlashInvaders has drawn comparisons to Pokémon GO, an augmented-reality app, though it was created two years earlier, in 2014. Both games encourage players to go outside in pursuit of their favourite monster or mosaic. The app also fits with a wider trend to merge digital and physical play for children, from app-assisted board games to LEGO bricks with built-in chips that respond to sound and movement. Yet FlashInvaders is free: the app is purely a passion project and makes no money.
paragraphs.zh: FlashInvaders 常被拿来与增强现实应用《Pokémon GO》作比较，尽管它更早于两年、也就是2014年推出。两款游戏都鼓励玩家走出室外，去追逐自己最喜欢的怪物或马赛克。该应用也符合更广泛的趋势：将数字和实体游戏结合给孩子们玩，从手机辅助的桌游到内置芯片、能对声音和动作作出反应的乐高积木。尽管如此，FlashInvaders 是免费的：这款应用完全是出于兴趣而做，并且不以盈利为目的。

paragraphs.en: The app stands out because it encourages people to “look at their environment in a different way”, notes Mr Schacter. Mobile games are often criticised for glueing people to their phones, but this one teaches you to take notice of the world around you. Whether on a little-known street or at the top of the Eiffel Tower, it’s always worth looking up. ■
paragraphs.zh: Schacter先生指出，这款应用之所以突出之处，在于它鼓励人们“用不同的方式看待周围环境”。人们常常批评手机游戏让玩家把自己“黏”在手机上，但这款游戏却教会你留意周围的世界。无论你在一条鲜为人知的街道，还是在埃菲尔铁塔顶上，抬头看看总是值得的。■

paragraphs.en: For more on the latest books, films, TV shows, albums and controversies, sign up to Plot Twist, our weekly subscriber-only newsletter
paragraphs.zh: 若想了解更多最新图书、电影、电视剧、专辑和争议话题，请订阅我们每周的付费通讯《Plot Twist》。
